title: nyc-commons — Geography
description: The shared geographic-identity vocabulary of New York City government. Every one of the 67 assessed domains carries some subset of these concepts (Borough is present in all 67; a Geography/GeographySpine block in 53). Factored from the Department of City Planning (DCP), which is the AUTHORITATIVE source of the city's boundaries — community districts, Neighborhood Tabulation Areas, census tracts, council/election districts, and the borough boundaries every other agency references. Domain `_common.json` files should `$ref` these definitions rather than re-declaring their own.

Borough.description: One of the five NYC boroughs. Open Data extracts spell this many ways ('Borough', 'BOROUGH', 'BORO', numeric 1-5); the canonical value is the full name.

BoroCode.description: DCP borough code: 1=Manhattan, 2=Bronx, 3=Brooklyn, 4=Queens, 5=Staten Island. The leading digit of every BBL and BIN.

Coordinates.description: A WGS84 point. Open Data delivers these as text (gis_latitude/longitude) or number (latitude/longitude) depending on the extract. Some agencies (e.g. NYPD) also carry the NY State Plane projection (xCoordinate/yCoordinate).

xCoordinate.description: NY State Plane (Long Island zone) X, where an agency carries it instead of / alongside WGS84.

yCoordinate.description: NY State Plane (Long Island zone) Y.

CommunityDistrict.description: A NYC Community District (Community Board area). DCP-defined (xi7c-iiu2). Referenced by nearly every land-use, service, and complaint record.

borocd.description: Borough + district as a single code, e.g. 101 = Manhattan CD 1.

CouncilDistrict.description: City Council district (1-51). Boundaries published by DCP; the geography every Council dataset joins on.

CensusTract.description: 2020 US Census tract as maintained by DCP (63ge-mke6).

NTA.description: Neighborhood Tabulation Area (2020 vintage) — DCP-defined aggregation of census tracts used for neighborhood-level demographics.

AdminBoundaries.description: The administrative-district overlays that a NYC location falls within, beyond the core land-use geography — the service districts agencies use to route work. Any subset may be present; open (agencies carry additional overlays such as taxi/sanitation micro-districts).

GeographySpine.description: THE shared location block of NYC government — the recurring key set that stitches records across agencies. DCP attaches it to a place; Buildings, Finance, Housing, Planning, Parks, DOE, and 311 all implicitly carry it. Make this the base every domain `$ref`s. All fields optional (a record carries whatever geography it knows); open (`additionalProperties`) so an agency's extra overlays — taxiZone, evacuationZone, sanitation micro-district — are never rejected.

communityBoard.description: Community district / community board number (PLUTO `community board`).

communityDistrict.description: Alias some agencies use for the community district / board.

nta.description: Neighborhood Tabulation Area code (2020 vintage), e.g. 'MN0101'.

neighborhood.description: Neighborhood name where an agency carries a text label rather than an NTA code.

zip.description: ZIP / postal code of the location.
